title: galium aparine var. tenerum
intro: Galium aparine var. tenerum, also known as sticky cleaver or cleaver's stick, is a variant of the Galium aparine plant, a perennial herb native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Galium aparine var. tenerum include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its potential to act as a diuretic and to soothe respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
